description: Two distinct `CORRUPTED_BANNER_STYLES` exports exist in the same feature folder with different — and inconsistent — values. Tests pass against the spec-correct one while the runtime renders with the spec-wrong one. (a) `promptnotes/src/lib/ui/app-shell/designTokens.ts:114-120` exports CORRUPTED_BANNER_STYLES with fontSize: '14px' and border: `1px solid {warnColor}` (i.e., `1px solid #dd5b00`). (b) `promptnotes/src/lib/ui/app-shell/corruptedBanner.ts:18-24` exports CORRUPTED_BANNER_STYLES with fontSize: '16px' and border: DESIGN_TOKENS.whisperBorder (i.e., `1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.1)`). REQ-014 acceptance criteria mandate fontSize 16px and Whisper Border (`1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.1)`). The unit test `corrupted-banner.unit.test.ts:124-132` imports from `corruptedBanner.ts` and asserts the SPEC-CORRECT values (16px, whisperBorder), so it passes. But `AppShell.svelte:10` imports `CORRUPTED_BANNER_STYLES` from `./designTokens.js` — i.e., the WRONG one — so the actually-rendered banner uses 14px and a #dd5b00 border. This is a regression of REQ-014 caused by the duplicate export collision, undetected by the green suite because the test imports the other (correct) symbol. Test coverage gap + structural duplication + REQ-014 violation in production rendering.
